name: arkime
label: Arkime
description: Arkime is an open-source, large-scale, full packet capture (FPC) and indexing system. It's designed for security professionals to store and analyze network traffic in detail.

fields.title: Server URL
fields.description: Specify the URL of the Arkime server to connect and perform automated operations.
fields.name: server_url
fields.tooltip: Specify the URL of the Arkime server to connect and perform automated operations.

fields.title: Username
fields.description: Specify the username to connect to the endpoint and perform automated operations
fields.name: api_id
fields.tooltip: Specify the username to connect to the endpoint and perform automated operations

fields.title: Password
fields.description: Specify the password to connect to the endpoint and perform automated operations
fields.name: api_secret
fields.tooltip: Specify the password to connect to the endpoint and perform automated operations

fields.title: Verify SSL
fields.description: Specifies whether the SSL certificate for the server is to be verified. By default, this option is selected, i.e., set to true.
fields.name: verify_ssl
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether the SSL certificate for the server is to be verified. By default, this option is selected, i.e., set to true.

operations.title: Get All Connections
operations.description: Retrieves a list of nodes and links from Arkime based on the filter parameters that you have specified.

parameters.title: Source Field
parameters.description: Specify the source database field name based on which you want to retrieve connections.
parameters.name: srcField
parameters.tooltip: Specify the source database field name based on which you want to retrieve connections.

parameters.title: Destination Field
parameters.description: Specify the destination database field name based on which you want to retrieve connections.
parameters.name: dstField
parameters.tooltip: Specify the destination database field name based on which you want to retrieve connections.

parameters.title: Baseline Date
parameters.description: Select the baseline date range to compare connections. Possible values are Disabled, 1× Query Range, 2× Query Range, 4× Query Range, etc. By default, this option is set as "Disabled".
parameters.name: baselineDate
parameters.tooltip: Select the baseline date range to compare connections. Possible values are Disabled, 1× Query Range, 2× Query Range, 4× Query Range, etc. By default, this option is set as "Disabled".

parameters.title: Baseline Visible
parameters.description: Select the connections to display when a baseline date range is applied. Possible values are All Nodes, Actual Nodes, Baseline Nodes, New Nodes, or Baseline Nodes Only. By default, this option is set as "All Nodes".
parameters.name: baselineVis
parameters.tooltip: Select the connections to display when a baseline date range is applied. Possible values are All Nodes, Actual Nodes, Baseline Nodes, New Nodes, or Baseline Nodes Only. By default, this option is set as "All Nodes".

parameters.title: Relative Time (Hours Ago)
parameters.description: Search from a specified number of hours ago until now. Use -1 to search all data. By default, this option is set as 1.
parameters.name: date
parameters.tooltip: Search from a specified number of hours ago until now. Use -1 to search all data. By default, this option is set as 1.

parameters.title: Search Expression
parameters.description: Specify the search expression string based on which you want to retrieve connections.
parameters.name: expression
parameters.tooltip: Specify the search expression string based on which you want to retrieve connections. Note: Must be URL encoded.

parameters.title: Arkime View
parameters.description: Specify the view name of the arkime to apply before the expression.
parameters.name: view
parameters.tooltip: Specify the view name of the arkime to apply before the expression.

parameters.title: Include Aggregations
parameters.description: Set to 1 to include aggregation info for maps and timeline graphs. By default, this option is set as 0.
parameters.name: facets
parameters.tooltip: Set to 1 to include aggregation info for maps and timeline graphs. By default, this option is set as 0.

parameters.title: Start DateTime
parameters.description: Select the DateTime using which you want to filter the result set to only include only those items that have been created after the specified timestamp. Note: Used if "Relative Time (Hours Ago)" is not set.
parameters.name: startTime
parameters.tooltip: Select the DateTime using which you want to filter the result set to only include only those items that have been created after the specified timestamp. Note: Used if "Relative Time (Hours Ago)" is not set.

parameters.title: Stop DateTime
parameters.description: Select the DateTime using which you want to filter the result set to only include only those items that have been created before the specified timestamp. Note: Used if "Relative Time (Hours Ago)" is not set.
parameters.name: stopTime
parameters.tooltip: Select the DateTime using which you want to filter the result set to only include only those items that have been created before the specified timestamp. Note: Used if "Relative Time (Hours Ago)" is not set.

parameters.title: Sort Order
parameters.description: Specify a comma-separated list of field names to sort the results by. Each field can optionally be followed by :asc for ascending or :desc for descending order.
parameters.name: order
parameters.tooltip: Specify a comma-separated list of field names to sort the results by. Each field can optionally be followed by :asc for ascending or :desc for descending order.

parameters.title: Fields to Return
parameters.description: Select one or more database field names to include in the response. Possible values are IP Protocol, Root ID, Total Data Bytes, etc
parameters.name: fields
parameters.tooltip: Select one or more database field names to include in the response. Possible values are IP Protocol, Root ID, Total Data Bytes, etc

parameters.title: Time Bounding
parameters.description: Select the time bounding mode for session search. Possible values are First Packet, Last Packet, Bounded (Both First and Last Inside Range), Session Overlaps Time Window, or Database (Stored Time Bounds). By default, this option is set as "Last Packet".
parameters.name: bounding
parameters.tooltip: Select the time bounding mode for session search. Possible values are First Packet, Last Packet, Bounded (Both First and Last Inside Range), Session Overlaps Time Window, or Database (Stored Time Bounds). By default, this option is set as "Last Packet".
options: Bounded (Both First and Last Inside Range)

parameters.title: Limit
parameters.description: Specify the maximum number of results, per page, that this operation should return. By default, this option is set as 100 and maximum is 2,000,000.
parameters.name: length
parameters.tooltip: Specify the maximum number of results, per page, that this operation should return. By default, this option is set as 100 and maximum is 2,000,000.

parameters.title: Offset
parameters.description: Index of the first item to be returned by this operation. This parameter is useful for pagination and for getting a subset of items. By default, this is set as 0
parameters.name: start
parameters.tooltip: Index of the first item to be returned by this operation. This parameter is useful for pagination and for getting a subset of items. By default, this is set as 0

operations.title: Get All Fields
operations.description: Retrieves a available database field objects pertaining to sessions from Arkime.

parameters.title: Array
parameters.description: If this option is selected, the response will return an array of fields, otherwise returns a map.

operations.title: Get All Unique Fields

parameters.description: Whether to return counts with he list of unique field values. Defaults to 0. 0 = no counts, 1 - counts.

parameters.tooltip: Whether to return counts with he list of unique field values. Defaults to 0. 0 = no counts, 1 - counts.

operations.title: Execute an API Request
operations.description: Sends an API request to an API endpoint based on specified HTTP method, endpoint, and other input parameters that you have specified, enabling flexible API interactions tailored to user needs.

parameters.name: method
parameters.title: HTTP Method
parameters.description:
Select an HTTP action for the request. You can select from the following options:  

DELETE 

GET 

PATCH 

POST 

PUT

parameters.title: Endpoint
parameters.name: endpoint
parameters.tooltip: Specify the target API URL path for example: connections
parameters.description: Specify the target API URL path for the request. For example, if the website is https://example.com and URL path is https://example.com/images/pic.jpg, the endpoint would be images/pic.jpg.

parameters.title: Query Parameters
parameters.name: query_params
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify any optional parameters to add to the URL and refine the request.

parameters.name: payload
parameters.title: Request Payload
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify data, as JSON, to be sent as the request payload (typically for POST or PUT requests).
